docs.text: Tasks Meta Development Servers for Students - Jack Will likely be following https://docs.computecanada.ca/wiki/Terraform Data Ingestion Ingestion of structures - Jack Need to figure out changes in CML since ~2020, and get some new data to play with. Search based on formula Search based on functional group Search based on number of ring atoms Search based on number of specific atoms Visuals Restore 2D/3D structure display - Tannia Restore spectra diagrams - Tannia Searching / Navigation Implement paging Clear search Next to the 'Search' button, add a new button, that is red, and states 'Clear'. When you click it, all of the search fields should be cleared. Clean-up of labels displayed in search results (ie. Not CML tags) - Aidan Implement selection options for information to be displayed in search results Add feature to download files (ex. Gaussian input file, Cartesians, molfile) - Tannia Sliders to search for items with numerical values Drop-down menus for search criteria with fixed options (ex. Basis set) Misc / Future Hide retrievium information in raw CML Users for retrievium.ca User upload of files – staging area Input SMILES of reactants and products and get delta H

docs.text: Using a virtual machine Getting the app running locally isn't trivial, so this is a method that will setup a virtual machine on (pretty much) any machine! There is still some things that need to be installed beforehand, but it is much less than the alternative . WARNING: This setup does not currently support Apple M1 processors, due to Virtual Box not running on anything but x86, you will have to follow the other tutorial for now. Setup 1 - Install Virtual Box 2 - Install Vagrant 3 - Install GitHub Desktop NOTE: If you prefer to use git via the cli / some other tool to use GitHub, feel free to, but if you don't have a preference or are new to GitHub, I would recommend you install GitHub Desktop. 4 - Clone Repository using GitHub Desktop Head to https://github.com/retrievium/retrievium and follow the above instructions to clone the repository to your local machine. If you can't access the above repo, you will need to reach out to someone to get your account added to the retrievium organization. 5 - Create virtual machine This one will likely take a while, but hopefully should require no interaction from you! cd /place/you/cloned/retrievium/iac-v1/vagrant/dev vagrant up If for some reason during this it fails, it might be due to a fluke of ansible, so you can run vagrant provision in the same directory again, and it will run the same scripts to download / setup all the software. 6 - Run script to start tmux session NOTE: This command should be ran inside your virtual machine, you can enter it by first heading into the iac-v1/vagrant/dev directory, and then running vagrant ssh ```sh cd /place/you/cloned/retrievium/iac-v1/vagrant/dev vagrant ssh ``` # within VM cd /vagrant/ make tmux 7 - Run script to add sample data Once you have ran the above step, tmux should be running. At the bottom of the screen you should see a green bar that starts with [ret] 0:core 1:caddy... , you can switch to the different panes by using Ctrl + B + <NUMBER> . Use Ctrl + B + 5 to head to the last pane (named terminal), and run: cd /vagrant make data 8 - Visit website in your browser! Open up http://localhost:2015/ in your web browser, the app should be running, and also should have data! You can check this by visiting the search page, and just hitting search. There should be data returned. 9 - Verify you can make changes to the website, and the website will reflect them. With your editor of choice, open up the repository. ( VSCode is a good choice if you don't have one already) Head to frontend-v1/src/components/particles_banner/ParticlesBanner.jsx , and change the line: <Title style={{ fontWeight: "400", textAlign: "center" }}> retrievium </Title> To: <Title style={{ fontWeight: "400", textAlign: "center" }}> Hi, my name is YOUR_NAME </Title> After saving the file, the homepage of the website should automatically refresh with your changes. Tips TODO tips about general usage shutting down the virtual machine disconnecting from tmux without shutting down the app attaching back to tmux again destroying the virtual machine
docs.title: Using a virtual machine

docs.text: Getting started WARNING: a lot of work has been put into making 'Using a Virtual Machine' the best way get the app running on any machine as fast as possible. while you are welcome to follow the bellow instructions, you should probably at least try the other method first! The goal of this section is to take you from nothing, to entire app running. Environment The install process will reference Unix commands you should run within code blocks, therefore it is expected that you are running such an environment. If you are: Running Linux, you are fine. Running MacOS, you are likely fine, but there might be some differences here and there with how things operate. Running Windows, you should likely check out the WSL section here beforehand, so you can spin up a Linux-like environment within Windows beforehand. If you run into a problem with something here, and find the solution, please contribute it back to the documentation to help those in the future! Overview of steps Clone the repository: git clone git@github.com:retrievium/retrievium.git cd retrievium Copy .env.dist to .env in the root folder of the project. cp .env.dist .env Follow the instructions in ops-v1 to sort out Docker / Caddy / Databases / etc . You should have Minio, ElasticSearch, Faktory, and Postgres running, along with Caddy before proceeding to the next step. Follow the instructions in api-v1 to setup the backend API . You should be able to run the API before proceeding to the next step, as well as ran the script to populate the ElasticSearch indexes. Follow the instructions in gulper-v1 to setup the ingestion pipeline / worker . You should have data in your database after this step, which means you have uploaded the sample tar, ran the worker, and had the worker process said sample tar into Minio / ElasticSearch / Postgres. Follow the instructions in frontend-v1 to setup the frontend You should be running the development build of the React application before proceeding to the next step. Visit http://localhost:2015/ in your browser, everything should be running! Troubleshooting If you made it to the last step, but you don't see the homepage, double check if you are: Running all of the required docker containers noted in ops-v1 , along with Caddy too. Have populated the databases with sample data. Running the API. Running the frontend. If that isn't the case / you have gotten stuck part way through, feel free to reach out in the Retrievium Discord server, for (hopefully) assistance from previous developers. If you are not in this discord server, reach out to the professors for access.
docs.title: Getting started

docs.text: Windows Subsystem for Linux It might be possible to run the application somewhat within Windows, but that is not to be discussed within this documentation. Preferable instead is installing WSL . And to increase your productivity with such a setup, you can: Setup VS Code to remote into and manage your WSL environment for you. Install Docker Desktop for managing containers. With the above things installed, you should be able to follow the getting started tutorial as-is from the Linux command line provided by WSL. As always, if you run into issues, make sure to ask for help, and if you figure things out, leave notes in the docs for future developers!
docs.title: Windows Subsystem for Linux

docs.text: It might be possible to run the application somewhat within Windows, but that is not to be discussed within this documentation. Preferable instead is installing WSL . And to increase your productivity with such a setup, you can: Setup VS Code to remote into and manage your WSL environment for you. Install Docker Desktop for managing containers. With the above things installed, you should be able to follow the getting started tutorial as-is from the Linux command line provided by WSL. As always, if you run into issues, make sure to ask for help, and if you figure things out, leave notes in the docs for future developers!

docs.text: Running api-v1 locally API for accessing the retrievium data. The frontend will talk to this API to access data, nothing outside of the backend should ever talk directly to the databases, the API is the layer that abstracts them. Setup cd api-v1 Required dependencies Node 17+ Node Version Manager You should use Node Version Manager to install Node, such that you can get the most recent stable version of it. Install projects Node version, and install the required NPM packages nvm use npm install Populate the ElasticSearch CML index IMPORTANT: assuming you have already setup ElasticSearch, explained in ops-v1 , run the following command after you have done the above to generate the index ElasticSearch will use to store CML files. npm run elasticsearch:generate-index Running the api Requirements: Running all core services (Caddy and the required docker containers noted in ops-v1 ) nvm use npm start The API should now be running behind Caddy at http://localhost:2015/api/v1/ .
docs.title: Running api-v1 locally

docs.text: Running frontend-v1 locally React frontend to interface with api-v1. Setup cd frontend-v1 Required dependencies Note: api-v1 asks you to install these things, if you have already done this you should be good to skip installing again . Node 17+ Node Version Manager You should use Node Version Manager to install Node, such that you can get the most recent stable version of it. Install projects Node version, and install the required NPM packages nvm use npm i Initialize secrets cp .env.dist .env Running the frontend Requirements: Running Caddy with local as in ops-v1 npm run start Note: create-react-app will stat listening on localhost:3000, but DO NOT use this to view the page, since it will fail making API requests due to CORS. The frontend should now be served by Caddy at http://localhost:2015/ Building the frontend npm run build
docs.title: Running frontend-v1 locally

docs.text: Running gulper-v1 locally Data ingestion worker to populate the databases. Setup cd gulper-v1 Required dependencies Python 3.9+ pg_config Installing pg_config On Ubuntu, simply run sudo apt install libpq-dev , as see this StackOverflow post . Create and enter virtual environment https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/venv.html python -m venv venv # make sure not python2, or python less than 3.9! . ./venv/bin/activate # or something similar to your system pip install -r requirements.txt Ensure install works python cli.py # should show cli tool help, without errors Populating the databases with sample data python cli.py uploadsampletar # upload sample tar to minio python cli.py listjobs # should show the job just added python cli.py queuejobs # queue jobs in faktory for processing python gulper.py # run the worker to process said jobs
docs.title: Running gulper-v1 locally

docs.text: Running ops-v1 locally Ops contains files required to run all of the services that the projects used that we don't develop ourselves. Setup cd ops-v1 Required dependencies Docker Docker Compose Caddy Installing Caddy cd caddy sh download_caddy.sh # downloads the linux build of caddy, for amd64 platforms If you are running production and wish Caddy to be able to bind on ports 80 / 443, run the following script: sh allow_caddy_bind.sh It will allow caddy to listen on ports below 1024 without sudo Populating secrets Before the first run, the secrets folder must be populated. cd core sh create_secrets.sh Populating Docker images / volumes Before the first run, Docker expects certain volumes to be created externally. cd core sh create_volumes.sh Running core services in development Running Caddy cd caddy sh start_caddy.sh local # run caddy, expecting to run on localhost on port 2015 Running core services cd core docker-compose up -d Potential issues ElasticSearch memory error Elasticsearch seems to bail before starting in Linux due to something relating this issue . Thankfully the fix is easy, and contained within this repository for ease-of-access. cd core sh es_mem_fix.sh
docs.title: Running ops-v1 locally

docs.title: Running Caddy

docs.text: cd core docker-compose up -d
docs.title: Running core services
